name: github-pr-review
description: Polls a GitHub repo for newly opened PRs and proposes review notes
prompt:
A pull request was just opened. The payload is:

{githubEvent}

Walk through it as a thoughtful first-pass reviewer:
1. Summarize the change in 1–2 sentences (what + why, inferred from the title, body, and changed files).
2. Note any security, performance, or correctness risks based on the diff scope.
3. List concrete review questions worth asking the author before merge.
4. Suggest a recommendation: ready-for-detailed-review, needs-clarification, or blocking-concerns.

Keep it under ~200 words. Edit the `repo` field of this template before installing — it ships pointing at `owner/name`.
